candidates.rationale: Opens with the cost crisis in multi-agent deployments: token costs scale super-linearly with topology complexity, yet practitioners pick topologies blindly with no cost awareness. Builds urgency before revealing CostRouter as the solution. Strong for ICML reviewers who value clear problem motivation and practical significance.

sections.title: Introduction
subsections: 1.1 The Cost Problem in Multi-Agent LLM Deployments
sections.argumentation_note: Open with concrete dollar-cost example: running a 3-agent debate topology on GPT-4 costs 6-12x a single call, yet delivers <10% accuracy gain on easy queries.

sections.title: Related Work
sections.argumentation_note: Organize into 4 thematic clusters.

sections.title: CostRouter: Cost-Aware Topology Routing
sections.argumentation_note: Formalize cost-quality tradeoff as constrained optimization.

sections.title: Experimental Setup
sections.argumentation_note: State 3 research questions.

sections.title: Results
sections.argumentation_note: Lead with main results table showing 40-60% cost reduction.

sections.title: Discussion
sections.argumentation_note: Interpret topology dominance by difficulty level.

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.argumentation_note: Recap contributions. Highlight 40-60% cost savings.

candidates.rationale: Opens with the key insight that topology cost is a structural property independent of the LLM backbone, meaning cost multipliers are predictable before execution. This reframes the problem from which model to which structure and makes CostRouter feel like the inevitable next step. Strongest novelty positioning for ICML originality criterion.

sections.title: Introduction
sections.argumentation_note: Open with the insight: a debate topology with 3 agents and 4 rounds costs 24x a single call regardless of the backbone LLM.

sections.title: Related Work
sections.argumentation_note: Frame related work through the lens of the insight.

sections.title: CostRouter: Topology-Level Cost Routing
sections.argumentation_note: Start from topology cost algebra.

sections.title: Experimental Setup
sections.argumentation_note: Frame experiments around validating the insight.

sections.title: Results and Analysis
sections.argumentation_note: First validate cost algebra. Then main comparison table.

sections.title: Discussion
sections.argumentation_note: Return to opening insight and confirm experiments validated it.

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.argumentation_note: Restate insight. Summarize 3 contributions.

candidates.rationale: Opens with a surprising empirical finding: on easy queries, a single LLM call matches a 3-agent debate at 1/24th the cost, while on hard queries the debate is worth every token. Builds the paper around explaining and exploiting this observation. High engagement hook but risks feeling purely empirical without theoretical grounding.

sections.title: Introduction
sections.argumentation_note: Open with motivating scatter plot.

sections.title: Related Work

sections.title: From Evidence to Router: CostRouter
sections.argumentation_note: Start from empirical observation.

sections.title: Experimental Evaluation
sections.argumentation_note: Experiments designed to generalize the opening observation.

sections.title: Results
sections.argumentation_note: Show difficulty-cost pattern holds on all benchmarks.

sections.title: Discussion
sections.argumentation_note: Synthesize evidence. Routing helps most with heterogeneous difficulty.

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.argumentation_note: Return to opening evidence.
